name: performance-testing-review-multi-agent-review
description: Use when working with performance testing review multi agent review
capabilities: Coordinates multiple specialized AI agents for parallel code analysis
Routes code to appropriate reviewers based on code characteristics
Consolidates findings from security, architecture, and performance agents
Resolves conflicting recommendations using weighted scoring
Supports file paths, Git repositories, and code snippets as input
author: sickn33
securitySummary: All 27 static analysis findings are false positives. The SKILL.md file is documentation containing Python pseudo-code examples in markdown blocks, not executable code. No actual command execution, cryptographic operations, or system reconnaissance patterns exist. Safe for publication.
valueStatement: Traditional code reviews miss critical issues by examining code from a single perspective. This tool coordinates multiple specialized AI agents to provide comprehensive analysis across security, architecture, performance, and quality dimensions.
userTitle: Orchestrate multi-agent code reviews with AI
limitations: Requires clear code context for accurate agent routing
Review quality depends on agent specialization coverage
Large codebases may require batch processing for complete analysis
Does not replace human judgment for final approval decisions

useCases.title: Web Application Security Review
useCases.description: Automatically route web application code through security auditors and web architecture specialists for comprehensive vulnerability assessment.

useCases.title: Microservices Architecture Validation
useCases.description: Validate microservices designs through sequential review phases covering design patterns, implementation quality, and deployment readiness.

useCases.title: Performance-Critical Code Analysis
useCases.description: Identify performance bottlenecks by routing performance-sensitive code through specialized performance analyst agents.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Code Review
promptTemplates.prompt: Review the code at [file path] using the multi-agent review system. Focus on identifying any critical issues and provide a summary of findings.

promptTemplates.title: Security-Focused Review
promptTemplates.prompt: Perform a security audit on [repository URL] using the security-auditor agent. Identify potential vulnerabilities and provide remediation recommendations.

promptTemplates.title: Full Architecture Review
promptTemplates.prompt: Conduct a comprehensive architecture review of [project path] using sequential agents: architect-reviewer for design patterns, code-quality-reviewer for implementation, and devops-validator for deployment considerations.

promptTemplates.title: Custom Agent Configuration
promptTemplates.prompt: Configure a custom review with parallel agents [security-auditor, performance-analyst] and sequential agent [architecture-reviewer] for [code snippet]. Weight security at 0.4, performance at 0.4, and architecture at 0.2.

bestPractices: Define clear review objectives before selecting agent types
Use parallel execution for independent review dimensions to reduce turnaround time
Review consolidated reports and escalate conflicting recommendations for human judgment
antiPatterns: Running all agent types on every review without considering code characteristics
Ignoring agent confidence scores when prioritizing findings
Skipping sequential dependency phases when agent outputs build on each other

faq.question: What types of code can this tool review?
faq.answer: The tool accepts file paths, Git repository URLs, and direct code snippets. It automatically detects code characteristics to route to appropriate specialized agents.

faq.question: How does the multi-agent system resolve conflicting recommendations?
faq.answer: A conflict resolution engine applies weighted scoring based on agent expertise and context relevance. Complex conflicts are escalated for human review.

faq.question: Can I customize which agents participate in a review?
faq.answer: Yes, you can specify custom agent configurations with weights for each agent type based on your review priorities.

faq.question: How long does a typical review take?
faq.answer: Review time varies by codebase size and agent count. Parallel agents run concurrently, while sequential phases wait for previous outputs. Most reviews complete within minutes.

faq.question: Does this tool integrate with existing CI/CD pipelines?
faq.answer: The tool supports Git repository inputs and can be invoked from CI/CD workflows. Output reports can be formatted for integration with common code quality platforms.

faq.question: What happens if an agent times out or fails?
faq.answer: The system implements fallback mechanisms with intelligent timeouts. Failed agents are logged and the review continues with remaining agents, noting gaps in coverage.
